title: phlox divaricata subsp. laphamii
intro: Phlox divaricata subsp. laphamii, also known as Lapham's phlox, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae, native to the Midwestern United States and the Great Lakes region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which have been used to treat respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Lapham's phlox include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve coughs and congestion, and promote wound healing. The herbal preparations of this plant can be found in various forms, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties for therapeutic purposes.
